message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first distributed agent shutdown-sleep poll (tick 282)

Migrates the shutdown-aware sleep loop in
`distributed_agent_sleep_with_shutdown` to use `sleep_with_cx` instead
of ambient `sleep`:

- `runtime_compat::sleep(...).await` →
  `sleep_with_cx(&sleep_cx, ...).await` with cancel-detection that
  short-circuits the wait loop on cx-cancel (treating it as shutdown-
  equivalent and returning `true`).

The fn's purpose is a poll-loop that sleeps up to `duration`, waking
every ≤250ms to re-check the shutdown_flag. Previously the ambient
`sleep` had to complete each 250ms slice before the next shutdown-flag
check. Now the cx-first variant gates each sleep slice on
`cx.checkpoint()` so a cx-cancel (e.g., from the outer distributed
agent task) aborts immediately at the next wake-up boundary without
waiting for the current slice to finish.

Semantically identical to the shutdown-flag behavior: `sleep_with_cx`
returning Err means cancel fired → treat as shutdown → return true.
The caller's loop logic is unchanged.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 96 (187-282).
main.rs cross-crate migration total: 174 sites (173 before + 1).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 47 (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
